title: T-tau Representation
description: Schoenfeld & Cole (1972) time-based parameterization. Represents time-dependent schedules as recurring cycles of length T with a reinforcement window of τ seconds.
reference: Schoenfeld, W. N., & Cole, B. K. (1972). Stimulus schedules: The t-τ systems. Harper & Row.

T.description: Cycle duration in seconds. Must be strictly positive.

tau.description: Reinforcement window in seconds. Must satisfy 0 < τ ≤ T.

is_available.description: Reinforcement availability predicate at elapsed time t.

exclusion_error.description: Ratio schedules define reinforcement as a function of response count, not elapsed time. Conversion requires an IRT assumption (see approximate_conversion).

information_loss: domain: FI and FT collapse to same representation

from_ttau.description: T-tau → Lattice conversion rules. Requires mandatory disambiguation parameters.

domain.description: Interval or Time. Resolves FI/FT ambiguity.

distribution.description: Fixed, Variable, or Random. Resolves distribution ambiguity.

rules.note: Sub-unity duty cycle maps to Limited Hold modifier. Temporal positioning differs from Schoenfeld's original (window at cycle start vs. after interval).

roundtrip.holds_when: S is Interval or Time schedule with duty_cycle == 1.0 and disambiguation params match original

approximate_conversion.description: Separated layer for Ratio → T-tau under explicit IRT assumption.

irt.description: Assumed mean inter-response time in seconds. No default — caller must provide.

approximate_conversion.note: IRT is a dependent variable, not a schedule parameter. The assumption is preserved in the result type for traceability.
